titleStmt.title: SII 25.166: original edition by M. D. Sampath
No. 166. (A.R. No. 166 of 1908.) TIRUNAṞAIYŪR, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.

summary.p: South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Siddhanāthasvāmi temple.
This inscription gives the details of the date viz., year 2, Kuṁbha 21, ba 1, Wednesday, Pūram which regularly corresponds to 1150 A.D., February 13.

It records the gift of 100 kāśu for maintaining a perpetual lamp to god Siddhīśvaram-uḍaiyār of [Tirunaṟaiyūr] in Tirunaṟaiyūr-nāḍu in Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu by Ekavāchagaṉ Kulōttuṅgaśōḻaṉār alias Vāṇakōvaraiyar of a place (name lost) included in Tuṉḍa-nāḍu in Muḍigoṇḍaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu. The Śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple undertook the poliyūṭṭu amount and agreed to maintain the lamp.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
kkaravattikaḷ śrīkulottuṅka coḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu varuṣattu kum
Irupattoṉṟāntiyatiyum peṟṟa Aparapakṣattu pratamaiyum putanki
m piratamaiyum peṟṟa pūrattu nāḷ kulottuṅka coḻavaḷanāṭṭut tirunaṟaiyūr nāṭṭu
Uṭaiyār cittiśvara muṭaiyāṟku vaṭakarai muṭikoṇṭa coḻavaḷanāṭṭut tuṇṭanāṭṭut tu
Uṭaiyāṉ Ekavācakaṉ kulottuṅka coḻaṉārāṉa vāṇakovaraiyar cantirātittavaṟ cella �
tirununtāviḷakku Oṉṟukku paiyamākat tiruviḷakkerikka Ikkoyiṟ kāṇi
ya civappirāmaṇan kāciyapaṉ piramiśvaramuṭaiyān Āṭkoṇṭa nāya
ṉum pāratāyaṉ marutāntaka tevaṉāṉa Āṭaviṭaṅkapaṭṭaṉum Āttiraiyan
vāḷaṉ Irā[ja*]rā[ja*] paṭṭaṉum Āttiraiyaṉ nampi tillaināyaka paṭṭaṉum Āttiraiyaṉ viru
cevaṭi nāṟpatteṇṇāyira paṭṭaṉum Āttiraiyaṉ civanta Aḷi nāyakaṉ cittiśvaramuṭai
yāṉum mokiliyaṉ tevaṉ Eḻunūṟṟuvapaṭṭanum Āttirai
yaṉ Āmpal niṟaicoti tiruvalañcuḻi yuṭaiyāṉum
Ivvaṉaivom cantirātittavaṟ cella Erikkak kaṭa
vatāṉa tirununtāviḷakkukku kaikkoṇṭa kācu 100 I
kkācu nūṟṟukkum kācu Oṉṟukku muppatu vaṭṭa
policaiyāl vanta kku Eṟṟic curukki nām Ippalicaiye ko
yilukku Erikka kaṭavomāka Upaiyam kuṭuttamaikku kalveṭṭu .
Digital edition of SII 25.166 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
